# Extracao: transcription_mp4_7__Always_Agree_With_The_Buyer

You know the first rule in selling is always, always, always agree with the buyer. And this is one of the vital rules in the close. Always agree with the buyer and never disagree. Disagreement will not help you in the close. Now there will, there will come a time when you're going to disagree but you always want to start with agreement, okay? So there is a point where I'm going to tell somebody, hey man, look you're not right about that, okay? That can't happen. But first, you have to build a lot of agreement prior to that. This is one of the absolute truths when negotiating. If you violate agreement early on, you'll crush your negotiations and you'll crush the close. You won't, it won't happen. Always always, always agree with their prospect no matter what. Now that's different than the old saying, the customer's always right. The customer is not always right, okay? Well, granted I can do this or I can do that or I can, how do you know that? That's not true. You can't do that, okay? They're not always right but that has nothing to do with telling them you're right. You get it? Whether the buyer's right or wrong is not the issue here, okay? When you're negotiating and going for a close, conflict has to get resolved so that an agreement can take place. To resolve conflict and disagreement, it requires one person not two. And now I'm thinking about a situation I'm involved in right now. I'm like, man, I need some help from the other side because I'm having trouble resolving this. If I could truly, truly take responsibility for everything going on in negotiations, you know, if I would just step out of the way of disagreement, there wouldn't be anything left to disagree with. It takes two parties to counter one another, okay? As soon as this guy agrees with him, there's no disagreement. For disagreement to exist, it requires two sides. Once one side agrees with the other, disagreement, shh, vanquished, over. That only results in not closing deals. It doesn't get you deals. You're going to lose the battle and win the war, okay? So how do I agree and still close? I know the price is too high, sir. I agree with you that the price is too high. I'm acknowledging and agreeing at the same time. I agree with you. I got it. Let me make a suggestion. What's that? Let's pay for it over time. Price won't be the problem, huh? And I'm using a smile. Hey, I agree with you, sir. I would want my wife involved as well. But if your wife is anything like my wife, she knows you're here right now. She knows what you're doing, okay? You guys have already discussed your budget and what you're going to be looking at. Come on, man. Let's roll. See what I'm doing? Always agree, always agree, eliminate disagreement from the close, and you'll close more deals.
